_meta.description: Embodied carbon cap (kgCO2e/m²) determined by the selected carbon benchmarking standard. For TGS4 Tier 2/3, the cap is looked up from the adopted City of Toronto values by building category (occupancy + storeys). For the legacy TGS4 'Typical Values', the cap comes from the material-based typology. For other standards, fixed targets or user-modelled values are used.
_meta.terminology: Use 'embodied' for emissions released to atmosphere. Use 'storage' for carbon sequestered in materials. Do not use 'embedded'. — Ryan Zizzo, Mantle Climate

inputs.name: building.typologyEmbodiedCarbon
inputs.description: Material-based typology cap from TYPOLOGY_CAPS lookup (i_39)

inputs.name: building.majorOccupancy
inputs.description: OBC Major Occupancy classification (d_12) — used for TGS4 Tier 2/3 only

inputs.name: building.numStoreys
inputs.description: Number of storeys (d_16_storeys) — used for TGS4 Tier 2/3 only

output.note: Returns 'N/A' when standard is 'Not Reported' or when no cap exists (e.g., Part 9 + Tier 3)

preconditions.condition: building.carbonStandard is a valid dropdown value
preconditions.description: Must be one of the supported standards

invariants.description: Embodied carbon caps are always positive

invariants.condition: TGS4 Tier 2/3 caps do NOT vary by material type
invariants.description: The City of Toronto did not adopt the material-specific Figure 1 values

behavior.condition: standard == 'TGS4 Tier 2' AND category == 'Part 9 Low-Rise Residential'
behavior.note: LCA scope A1-A3. Structure and enclosure.
LCA scope A1-A5. Structure and envelope.
LCA scope A1-A5. Structure and envelope.

behavior.condition: standard == 'TGS4 Tier 3' AND category == 'Part 9 Low-Rise Residential'
behavior.note: Part 9 has no Tier 3 target in TGS4.
LCA scope A1-A5. Structure and envelope.
LCA scope A1-A5. Structure and envelope.
Backward compat: uses material-based TYPOLOGY_CAPS (Figure 1 values, not adopted by City)

behavior.condition: standard in CARBON_TARGETS (LEED, Passive House, CaGBC, etc.)
behavior.note: Fixed caps: LEED BD+C v4.1 = 500, LEED 40% = 300, ZCB = 250, PH = 200, CaGBC = 175

edgeCases.condition: TGS4 Tier 3 selected for Part 9 building
edgeCases.rationale: Part 9 Low-Rise Residential has no Tier 3 embodied carbon target in TGS4

edgeCases.condition: Bare 'TGS4' from old saved state / CSV import
edgeCases.handling: Falls through to legacy typology path (backward compat)
edgeCases.rationale: Old files saved with 'TGS4' still work via material-based TYPOLOGY_CAPS

edgeCases.condition: Occupancy changes while TGS4 Tier selected
edgeCases.rationale: Category changes when occupancy changes (e.g., residential → commercial)

edgeCases.condition: Storey count changes while TGS4 Tier selected for C-Residential
edgeCases.rationale: Part 9 vs Part 3 boundary is at 3 storeys for residential

tests.name: TGS4 Tier 2 — Part 9 low-rise residential (2 storeys)
TGS4 Tier 2 — Part 9 low-rise residential (3 storey boundary)
TGS4 Tier 2 — Part 3 mid-rise residential (4 storey boundary)

references.source: City of Toronto TGS Version 4 — Part 3 Mid-to-High Rise
City of Toronto TGS Version 4 — Part 9 Low-Rise Residential
City of Toronto TGS Version 4 — City-Owned Buildings
Ryan Zizzo, Mantle Climate — clarification on adopted vs Figure 1 values
references.note: The caps do NOT vary by material type (ie: steel, concrete, wood)
